questionHtml: The apparatus used to measure energy content of food contains some water. Explain how this water is used when measuring the energy content of a food.
markschemeHtml:
a. the volume/mass of water is measured;

b. the energy from the burning food raises the temperature/heats the water;

c. knowing the specific heat of water allows energy of the food to be calculated
OR
4.2J of heat energy causes a 1°C rise in 1g of water;

meta.title: The apparatus used to measure energy content of food contains some water. Explain how this water is used when measuring the energy content of a food.
